data.title: Smart Share Global receives preliminary acquisition proposal from Hillhouse
data.description:
• Smart Share Global (NASDAQ:EM) announced on Friday that the board of directors received a preliminary non-binding proposal letter from Hillhouse Investment Management, to acquire all the outstanding ordinary shares of the company that are not already beneficially owned by Hillhouse or the Management Members for $1.77 per ADS or $0.885 per share in cash.
  • The company earlier announced that it had entered into a definitive Agreement and plan of merger, pursuant to which the company will be acquired by a consortium includes Trustar Mobile Charging.
  • "The special committee of the company’s independent directors, which was formed following receipt of a preliminary non-binding proposal letter, dated January 5, 2025, from the Consortium will continue to consider and evaluate all options for the best interests of shareholders with the assistance of the special committee’s independent financial and legal advisors," said the company. 
  • The Board cautioned the company’s shareholders and others considering trading the company’s securities that no decisions have been made with respect to this proposal from Hillhouse. 
  • EM closed +0.42735% at $1.175.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Prologis registers mixed shelf offering
data.description:

data.title: Palisade Bio registers 8.9M shares for resale by selling stockholders
data.description:
• Palisade Bio (NASDAQ:PALI) filed to register up to 8.9M shares of common stock for resale by selling stockholders.
  • The shares include 8.64M issuable upon exercise of a warrant from a private placement, and 259,134 from placement agent warrants.
  • Palisade Bio will not receive any proceeds from the resale of these shares.

data.title: Wall Street ended mostly lower as investors eye Trump-Putin summit in Alaska
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages ended mostly lower on Friday, as market participants have been keeping an eye on the highly anticipated meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) closed slightly higher by 0.07%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) ended lower by 0.29%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite concluded in the red as well as it handed back 0.40%.

“Whilst overlevered late longs may be found consoling themselves with a spirit-laced Celsius this evening, the prudent investor will be celebrating another green week; indeed a week where the Dow finally cheered up and joined the party,” Seeking Alpha’s analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital Research stated. 

“The boomer wins of UNH and INTC ran hot to close the week, dragging Grandpa Dow onto the dancefloor; rather bizarrely due to (i) the prospect of high-fiving retail investors bidding up UNH to get Berkshire Hathaway somewhere back nearer cost and (ii) a potential federal bailout of Intel.  At least it makes a change from AI,” King added.

From a sector vantage point, only four of the S&P 500’s 11 segments ended trading in positive territory, with Health Care and Real Estate at the top of the leaderboard. In reverse, Financials were the weakest performer on the day. 

Over in the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields moved up as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) pushed up 3 basis point to 4.32%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) inched up 2 basis points to 3.75%. 

Attention is now focused on Alaska, where Trump and Putin have started talks on what's expected to focus around the Russia Ukraine war. En route to the summit, Trump stated on the possibility of territorial swaps: 

"They'll be discussed, but I've got to let Ukraine make that decision... I'm not here to negotiate for Ukraine. I'm here to get them at a table." 

On the economic calendar, July retail sales rose slightly less than expected and core sales came in line with forecasts. Import prices rose more than expected in July, and the export prices moved up. 

Industrial production edged down in July while manufacturing output remained unchanged.

Additionally, the U.S. consumer sentiment index slid to 58.6 in August from 61.7 in July, and business inventories rose as expected, in the month of June.

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of UnitedHealth (UNH) popped 12% after Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway disclosed in a regulatory filing that it purchased more than 5M shares of the insurer last quarter. 

Applied Materials (AMAT), meanwhile, tumbled 13.9% on the day after delivering fourth-quarter guidance that came in well below Wall Street’s expectations.

data.title: Standard Chartered under pressure as Stefanik asks for DoJ probe
data.description:
Standard Chartered (OTCPK:SCBFF) is down ~6% after Rep. Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) requested that the Department of Justice launch an investigation into the bank over allegations of dodging sanctions placed on it.

Those restrictions are in regards to allegedly violating Iranian sanctions and not following anti-money laundering procedures. The UK-based bank was fined $1.1B in 2019 from regulators in the US and UK.

Stefanik's urgency is due to a deferred-prosecution agreement set to end on Aug. 19.

Stefanik's letter to Attorney General Pam Bondi states that "Standard Chartered Bank made at least $9.6 billion in illicit payments to known terrorists. China has been using Standard Chartered Bank to purchase sanctioned Iranian oil.

"These payments were hidden from required disclosure under the current deferred prosecution agreement being supervised by the Southern District of New York and the U.S. Attorney for Washington D.C."

Stefanik also criticized New York Attorney General Letitia James for not taking action against Standard Chartered in 2024 despite the allegations.

She also requested that acting U.S. Attorney for New Jersey, Alina Habba, lead the probe.

Standard Chartered (OTCPK:SCBFY) told MarketWatch that the allegations are "entirely false."

data.title: U.S. drilling rig count stays flat after string of declines in Baker Hughes survey
data.description:
The number of total oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. remained unchanged at 539, after the count had declined in 14 of the previous 15 weeks, Baker Hughes reported Friday in its latest weekly survey.

Oil rigs added 1 to 412 during the week, while gas rigs fell by 1 to 122 and 5 rigs continued to be classified as miscellaneous.

The total rig count was down 47 rigs, or 8% below this time last year.

In Texas, the biggest oil and gas producing state, the total rig count fell by 1 to 242, and in the Permian Basin, the top U.S. oil-producing shale formation, the total rig count lost 1 to 255, the lowest since September 2021 for both areas.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: Bausch Health rises after $300M worth insider purchase (update)
data.description:
Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) shares added ~4% on Friday after John Paulson, a board member of the Canadian pharma giant, disclosed the purchase of more than $300M worth of company shares. Bausch + Lomb (BLCO), a Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) subsidiary in which Paulson holds another board position, also traded higher.

According to a regulatory filing on Thursday, Paulson, the President and Portfolio Manager of Paulson Capital, bought more than 34.7M shares of Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) stock for an average of $9.00 apiece in a transaction worth roughly $312.5M.

The transaction conducted on Thursday has lifted Paulson’s stake in the eye care company by 96%. A day earlier, he had purchased about 3.2M BHC shares for an average price of approximately $6.56 apiece in a deal valued at $21.3M.

Meanwhile, according to an amended 13-D filing, billionaire investor Carl Icahn has liquidated his entire stake in Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) with a sale of nearly 34.7M BHC shares on Thursday.

In a press release late Friday, Bausch Health (BHC) confirmed the transactions, noting that Paulson Capital Inc. and certain affiliates and funds managed by the firm acquired a net long position of about 34.7M shares held by Carl Icahn and his affiliates.

Paulson Capital and its affiliates now hold roughly 19% of the company’s common shares, and in connection with the deal, Brett Icahn and Steven Miller have resigned from Bausch Health’s (BHC) board.

Update 3.37 PM ET: Adds Bausch Health's update
